etymology.description: 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") + 寺(寺) (sì, "temple") = hand positioning something in a manner akin to being orderly as in a temple -> to hold or maintain
etymology.interpretation: The character 持 combines the semantic element 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") indicating a manual action, with the phonetic element 寺(寺) (sì, "temple") to suggest the orderly position associated with holding or maintaining something, as one might maintain a temple.

examples.chinese: 他一直持积极的态度(他一直持積極的態度)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yīzhí chí jījí de tàidù.
examples.english: He always maintains a positive attitude.

examples.chinese: 在这个团队中，大家都互相支持(在這個團隊中，大家都互相支持)。
examples.pinyin: Zài zhège tuánduì zhōng, dàjiā dōu hùxiāng zhīchí.
examples.english: In this team, everyone supports each other.

examples.chinese: 他能够持久地微笑(他能夠持久地微笑)，让人感到温暖(讓人感到溫暖)。
examples.pinyin: Tā nénggòu chíjiǔ de wēixiào, ràng rén gǎndào wēnnuǎn.
examples.english: He can maintain a lasting smile, making others feel warmth.
